methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Nano One Materials is a public-stage company (TSX: LNZA) in the Climate & Energy sector that has raised approximately C$100M. The comparable universe consists of 1 public peer and 8 private companies operating in adjacent frontier-energy and materials technology domains, including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

A peer-median valuation approach is not applicable in this case. Although 21 same-sector peers exist within Climate & Energy broadly, the company occupies a narrow technology niche with no peers meeting strict stage-alignment or valuation-tier criteria (minimum threshold: 3 datapoints). Filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables yielded zero qualifying matches, making an all-sector median comparison uninformative and potentially misleading.

The fragmented comp universe reflects Nano One's position at the intersection of battery-materials innovation and climate-technology infrastructure—a space where few public benchmarks and limited private-market comparability data exist. Any valuation conclusion would require alternative methods (revenue multiples, cost-of-capital approaches, or scenario DCF modeling) applied to company-specific financials not provided here. Without sufficient peer density or applicability of triangulation methods, quantitative range estimation cannot be supported by the available data.
